title: Tranquil Smoky French Chicken Blanquette
description: A light and smoky French dish featuring chicken, rice syrup, and jocheong, cooked to perfection in a steamy blanquette, this recipe is a unique twist on traditional French cuisine, with the added bonus of being kosher-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the rice syrup, jocheong, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the syrup mixture.
Transfer the chicken to a large blanquette dish and pour in the chicken broth.
Cover the dish with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, then remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the dish from the oven and stir in the heavy cream.
Return the dish to the oven and bake for a final 5 minutes, or until the cream has thickened and the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
